# **II. Software Design Document** {#ii.-software-design-document}

## **1\. System Design** {#1.-system-design}

### **1.1 System Architecture** {#1.1-system-architecture}

The Apartment Management Mobile Application follows a 2-tier Client-Server architecture. The Flutter mobile application acts as the client layer, while Firebase cloud services act as the backend layer. There is no custom application server in this system. The mobile app communicates directly with Firebase services through Firebase SDKs.

The system includes three main user roles: Admin, Staff, and Resident. Each role can access different features based on permission.

![][image2]

***Component Explanation*** 

| No | Component  | Description |
| :---: | ----- | ----- |
| 01 | Flutter Mobile Application | The client-side application used by Admin, Staff, and Resident. It provides user interfaces and handles basic business logic. |
| 02 | Admin Module | Allows the administrator to manage users, apartments, and announcements. |
| 03 | Staff Module | Allows staff members to manage resident requests, update request status, and manage service bills. |
| 04 | Resident Module | Allows residents to view announcements, submit requests, view bills, and manage their profile. |
| 05 | Firebase Authentication | Handles user login, registration, and authentication by email and password. |
| 06 | Cloud Firestore | Stores the main application data such as users, apartments, bills, requests, and notifications. |
| 07 | Firebase Storage | Stores uploaded files such as request images or profile images. |
| 08 | Firebase Cloud Messaging | Sends push notifications to users when new announcements or request updates are available. |

### **1.2 Package Diagram** {#1.2-package-diagram}

The system is organized into packages based on the responsibility of each part of the Flutter application. This structure helps separate UI, business logic, data models, and Firebase services. 

![][image3]

***Package Descriptions***

| No | Package | Description |
| :---: | ----- | ----- |
| 01 | models | Contains data model classes used to represent Firestore documents such as User, Apartment, Request, Bill, and Notification. |
| 02 | screens | Contains all UI screens of the application. Screens are divided by role: authentication, admin, staff, and resident. |
| 03 | screens/auth | Contains login and register screens. |
| 04 | screens/admin | Contains screens used by Admin to manage users, apartments, and notifications. |
| 05 | screens/staff | Contains screens used by Staff to manage resident requests and service bills. |
| 06 | screens/resident | Contains screens used by Resident to view notifications, submit requests, view bills, and update profile. |
| 07 | services | Contains classes that communicate with Firebase services such as Authentication, Firestore, Storage, and Cloud Messaging. |
| 08 | widgets | Contains reusable UI components such as buttons, text fields, and loading indicators. |
| 09 | utils | Contains common utilities such as constants, validators, and application routes. |
| 10 | main.dart | The entry point of the Flutter application. It initializes Firebase and starts the app. |

## **2\. Database Design** {#2.-database-design}

The system uses Cloud Firestore as the main database. Firestore is a NoSQL document database. Data is stored in collections and documents instead of traditional relational tables.   
![][image4]

***Table Descriptions***

| No | Table | Description |
| ----- | :---- | :---- |
| 01 | users | Stores information about all users in the system, including Admin, Staff, and Resident. \- Primary key: userId \- Foreign keys: apartmentId |
| 02 | apartments | Stores apartment information such as apartment number, floor, building, and owner. \- Primary key: apartmentId \- Foreign keys: ownerId |
| 03 | requests | Stores support or maintenance requests submitted by residents. Staff can update the status of each request. \- Primary key: requestId \- Foreign keys: residentId, apartmentId |
| 04 | bills | Stores monthly service bills of residents, including amount, type, due date, and payment status. \- Primary key: billId \- Foreign keys: apartmentId, residentId |
| 05 | notifications | Stores announcements created by Admin or Staff and displayed to users. \- Primary key: notificationId \- Foreign keys: createdBy |

## **3\. Detailed Design** {#3.-detailed-design}

### **3.1 Feature: Manage Maintenance Requests**  {#3.1-feature:-manage-maintenance-requests}

This feature allows Residents to submit maintenance or repair requests (e.g., broken water pipe, electrical issues) to the building management. Building Staff can view these requests and update their processing status (*Pending, In Progress, Completed*).

#### ***3.1.1 Class Diagram***

![][image5]

***3.1.2 Sequence Diagram: Create Repair Request*** 

![][image6]

#### **3.1.3 Use Case Specification: Create Repair Request**

* **Use Case ID:** UC\_REQ\_01  
* **Use Case Name:** Create Repair Request  
* **Actor:** Resident  
* **Description:** Allows a resident to submit a new maintenance request with descriptions and optional images.  
* **Pre-conditions:** Resident is successfully logged into the mobile application.  
* **Normal Flow:**  
  * Resident navigates to the "Maintenance Request" section.  
  * Resident clicks on the "Create New Request" button.  
  * System displays a form requesting: Title, Description, Category (Plumbing, Electrical, General), and an optional photo attachment.  
  * Resident fills in the details and submits.  
  * System validates the inputs, uploads the image (if any) to Firebase Storage, saves the request data into Firestore with status "Pending", and triggers a push notification to Building Staff.  
* **Alternative Flow (Cancel):**  
  * At step 4, if the Resident clicks "Cancel", the system discards the input and returns to the Request List screen.  
* **Exception Flow (Missing Fields):**  
  * At step 5, if required fields (Title or Description) are empty, the system displays `MSG02` ("The {field\_name} field is required.") and stops the submission.

#### **3.1.4 Use Case Specification: Update Request Status**

* **Use Case ID:** UC\_REQ\_03  
* **Use Case Name:** Update Request Status  
* **Actor:** Building Staff  
* **Description:** Allows staff to update the status of a request to keep the resident informed.  
* **Pre-conditions:** Staff is logged in and viewing the specific request details.  
* **Normal Flow:**  
  1. Staff reviews the request details.  
  2. Staff clicks on the "Update Status" dropdown.  
  3. Staff selects a new status (*In Progress* or *Completed*) and adds an optional resolution note.  
  4. Staff clicks "Save".  
  5. System updates the status in Firestore and sends a push notification to the respective Resident.

### **3.2 Feature: Manage Bills & Payments**

####  **Functional Overview**

This feature enables Building Staff to generate monthly bills (Management fees, Electricity, Water) for each apartment. Residents can view their outstanding/paid bills and proceed with payments.

#### **3.2.1Class Diagram**

![][image7]

#### **3.2.2 Sequence Diagram: Staff Creates New Bill** 

![][image8]

#### **3.2.3 Use Case Specification: Create Monthly Bill**

* **Use Case ID:** UC\_BILL\_02  
* **Use Case Name:** Create Monthly Bill  
* **Actor:** Building Staff  
* **Description:** Allows staff to create a new billing invoice for a specific apartment.  
* **Pre-conditions:** Staff is logged into the system.  
* **Normal Flow:**  
  * Staff navigates to the "Bill Management" tab and selects "Create Invoice".  
  * Staff inputs: Apartment ID, Bill Type (Electricity/Water/Service), Amount, Billing Month, and Due Date.  
  * Staff clicks "Generate Bill".  
  * System saves the bill document into Firestore with status "Unpaid" and sends a notification toast `MSG09` to the resident.  
* **Exception Flow (Invalid Amount):**  
  * At step 3, if the inputted amount is negative or non-numeric, the system displays an error message "Invalid amount value" and highlights the field.
